metadata.title: Mobile + User Flow + Platform Comparison
metadata.playlist: Make It Visible — Visual Identity & Wireframes
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 7 (Visual) brief — mobile wireframe, user flow, platform comparison; due 2026-07-17

beats.narration_text: Hola — Liam, Episode 5 of 6. Five desktop wireframes are done. Now three more deliverables: the mobile wireframe for the Home page, the user flow showing how a user gets from landing to completing the primary action, and a platform comparison for how you will build the actual site in A8. This episode is the decision episode — by the end of it you know which platform you are using for A8 and why, in writing.

props.command: Desktop wireframes done. Generate: mobile Home wireframe at 390px, 5-step user flow, and a Vercel v0 / Framer AI / Wix comparison with a platform recommendation.
props.runningText: mobile Home at 390px · 5-step user flow · platform comparison (3×3 table) · pick one and write the reason…
output: Mobile wireframe: Home page at 390px — hamburger nav, stacked sections, thumb-reach CTA
User flow: 5 steps from landing → primary action, drawn as labeled diagram in Figma
Platform comparison: Vercel v0 / Framer AI / Wix on 3 criteria + recommendation

beats.narration_text: The mobile wireframe is one page — the Home page — redrawn for a 390-pixel wide frame. Three changes you will almost always make. First: the navigation collapses to a hamburger menu icon. Second: sections that were side-by-side on desktop stack vertically on mobile. Third: image sections that required width to work visually either become cropped or are replaced by icon callouts. The rule for mobile wireframes: if a section does not pass the thumb-reach test — can a user reach it with their thumb while holding the phone in one hand — reconsider its placement. The primary CTA button should be reachable with one thumb. The wireframe convention for mobile is the same as desktop: gray rectangles, text labels, no color. Export the mobile frame as a PNG at 1x alongside the five desktop pages.

props.artifactHeading: 3 structural changes from desktop to mobile
artifactLines: 1. Navigation collapses to hamburger menu icon.
2. Side-by-side sections stack vertically.
3. Wide image sections: crop or replace with icon callouts.
Thumb-reach test: can the primary CTA be reached with one thumb?
Same convention as desktop: gray rectangles + text labels, no color.
Export: PNG at 1x, alongside the 5 desktop PNGs.
props.sparkLine: The primary CTA must pass the thumb-reach test. Everything else can move.

beats.act: the user flow — 5 steps from landing to action
beats.narration_text: The user flow is a five-step sequence showing how a new user gets from the landing page to completing the primary action of the product. Not the full user journey — just the first action. Step one: the user lands on the Home page. Step two: the user reads the hero and clicks the primary CTA. Step three: the user reaches the sign-up or try-it page — this might be the Contact page, a sign-up modal, or a product page with an embedded CTA. Step four: the user enters their information or takes the required action. Step five: the user sees a confirmation or success state. Five steps. The user flow in the wireframe context is a labeled diagram: five boxes connected by arrows, one sentence per box describing what the user does and what the UI shows. Draw it in Figma as a sixth frame beside the five page wireframes.

props.artifactTitle: User Flow — 5 Steps to Primary Action
artifactLines: Step 1: user lands on Home page.
Step 2: user reads hero + clicks primary CTA.
Step 3: user reaches sign-up / try-it page (Contact, modal, or embedded CTA).
Step 4: user enters information or completes the required action.
Step 5: user sees confirmation / success state.
In Figma: 6th frame — 5 boxes connected by arrows, 1 sentence per box.
props.sparkLine: 5 steps, 5 boxes, 5 arrows. The user flow is a 6th Figma frame beside the 5 pages.

beats.act: platform comparison — Vercel v0, Framer AI, Wix
beats.narration_text: Platform comparison: three platforms, three criteria. Vercel v0: an AI site generator that takes a prompt and produces production-grade React code. Best for students who are comfortable with code or who want a site that is technically impressive for their portfolio. Outputs real code deployable to Vercel in minutes. Framer AI: a no-code visual builder with AI layout generation. Best for students who want precise visual control without writing code. The output looks polished out of the box and requires less post-generation editing. Wix: the broadest no-code platform with the most templates. Best for students who want the most guardrails and the lowest technical risk. The output is less impressive technically but the platform handles hosting, forms, and domain management with zero configuration. Three criteria for comparison: ease of use for a non-developer, AI assist quality when given a brand brief, and cost for a solo student on the free tier.

props.artifactHeading: Vercel v0 · Framer AI · Wix — 3 criteria each
artifactLines: Vercel v0: prompt → production React code. Best for code-comfortable students or portfolio depth.
Framer AI: no-code visual builder with AI layout. Best for polished result, minimal editing.
Wix: broadest templates, most guardrails. Best for lowest technical risk.
Criterion 1: ease of use for a non-developer.
Criterion 2: AI assist quality from a brand brief + wireframe spec.
Criterion 3: cost for a solo student on the free tier.
props.sparkLine: The platform decision made here is the one you execute in A8. Pick it now, write the reason.

beats.narration_text: Platform recommendation for most INFO 7375 students: Framer AI. It accepts a brand brief and style guide directly, generates a multi-page site that closely matches wireframe specs, and produces a result that looks like a real product launch page rather than a homework project. It has a free tier that covers everything needed for A8. If you have strong coding skills and want to demonstrate technical depth for your portfolio, use Vercel v0 — the output is more impressive technically and the code is yours to show. Use Wix only if both Framer and Vercel feel like too much for the time you have — it will complete the assignment but will not distinguish the submission. Pick one platform now and write the reason in your A7 visual identity document. One sentence: 'I am using Framer AI because...' That decision and that sentence go into the A8 brief.

props.artifactHeading: Pick one. Write the reason. That decision goes into A8.
artifactLines: Most students: Framer AI. Accepts brand brief + style guide, looks like a real launch page.
Portfolio depth: Vercel v0. Real React code, deployable, showable.
Lowest technical risk: Wix. Gets the assignment done, won't distinguish the submission.
Pick one now. Write: 'I am using [platform] because [one sentence reason].'
That sentence goes directly into the A8 brief — don't reconstruct it later.
props.sparkLine: Framer AI for most students. Vercel v0 for portfolio depth. Decide now, write the reason.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. Mobile wireframe: Home page at 390 pixels, three structural changes — hamburger nav, stacked sections, thumb-reach CTA. User flow: five steps from landing to primary action, drawn as a labeled diagram in a sixth Figma frame. Platform comparison: Vercel v0 for code-comfortable students, Framer AI for most students, Wix for lowest technical risk. Pick one platform and write the reason. The platform decision made here is the one you execute in A8.

artifactLines: Mobile wireframe: Home at 390px — hamburger nav · stacked sections · thumb-reach CTA.
User flow: 5 steps, 6th Figma frame, 5 boxes + arrows.
Platform: Vercel v0 (code) · Framer AI (most students) · Wix (lowest risk).
Pick one. Write: 'I am using [platform] because [reason].' Save it for A8.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Here is the nina platform comparison prompt. Paste it with your product description and A7 visual deliverables in context. The prompt reads: Here is my product: [paste one-sentence description]. Here is my Home page wireframe spec: [paste the Home page section list]. Compare Vercel v0, Framer AI, and Wix on three criteria: ease of use for a non-developer, quality of AI-assisted layout generation from a brand brief and wireframe spec, and cost for a solo student on the free tier. Format as a three-row, three-column table. Add a one-sentence recommendation for a student whose primary goal is a polished-looking result with minimal technical risk. After running the prompt: if the recommendation matches the platform you were already planning to use, write it in your A7 document. If it does not, write one sentence explaining why you are overriding the recommendation. That override is a legitimate choice — just write it down before A8.

props.command: Here is my product: [one-sentence description]. Here is my Home page wireframe spec: [paste spec]. Compare Vercel v0, Framer AI, and Wix on 3 criteria: ease of use for a non-developer · AI layout quality from a brand brief · cost for a solo student on free tier. Format: 3-row × 3-column table. Add a 1-sentence recommendation for minimal-risk polished result.
props.runningText: if the recommendation matches your plan, write it down. If you're overriding, write the reason — legitimate choice, just document it before A8…

beats.narration_text: Mobile + User Flow + Platform Comparison. Liam, in for Bear.
